findings.evidence: Mathlib has `instance map (p : Submodule R M) [Module.Finite R p] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) : Module.Finite R (p.map f)` at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Finiteness/Basic.lean:276`; the PR’s `map_toSubmodule` at `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcoalgebra/Map.lean:76` makes `(A.map f).toSubmodule` exactly `A.toSubmodule.map f.toLinearMap`.
findings.fix: Delete this instance and rely on `Module.Finite.map`; if a named theorem is wanted, prove `map_finite` by `rw [map_toSubmodule]; infer_instance` rather than adding a parallel instance.
findings.issue: `instFiniteMap` re-registers finite generation of a submodule image, which is already supplied by Mathlib for `Submodule.map`.

summary: The subcoalgebra image construction itself is new, but the finite-generation instance duplicates Mathlib’s existing finite-submodule image instance.
